prompt: A cinematic, highly detailed editorial illustration of a pair of human lungs split into two contrasting halves. The left lung is formed from lush green forests, clean air, and soft natural light, symbolizing health and life. The right lung is constructed from dense urban city buildings, factories, and industrial structures releasing thick black smoke into the air. The smoke spreads gradually from the polluted lung into the healthy lung like an infection, blending into it. The boundary between the two lungs is uneven and organic, not symmetrical. Composition follows rule of thirds, with the polluted lung dominating slightly more space. Mood: dark, emotional, suffocating, and urgent. Color palette: vibrant greens on the left, deep greys and blacks on the right, with a subtle transition zone. Lighting is dramatic and cinematic, with strong shadows. No text, no labels, no legends, no axes, no numbers, no chart elements.
insight: Air pollution causes millions of deaths every year by slowly destroying human lungs.
concept: Air pollution visualized as a disease infecting human lungs
tradition_approach: Symbolic storytelling using contrast between healthy nature and polluted industrial environments
